$schema_note: Manifest of photographic assets the site needs. Hermes (image_gen) generates each entry to its exact `path`, overwriting in place so no code changes are required. Favicons + social cards are NOT here — they are vector-rasterized via `npm run gen:icons`. Style guide for every prompt: photorealistic, bright, professional Australian trade-business look, natural daylight, realistic colours, no text, no logos, no watermarks, not cheesy/stocky.
style: Photorealistic, bright, professional, trustworthy local Australian trade business. Natural daylight, clean modern suburban setting, crisp focus, realistic colours. No watermarks and no stray/garbled text. Equipment (motors, remotes, keypads, opener boxes) should carry the genuine 'MERLIN' brand wordmark where noted in the prompt — spelled exactly M-E-R-L-I-N and legible — but no OTHER brand logos.

images.purpose: Homepage hero background (text overlays the left side)
images.where_used: src/components/sections/Hero.tsx — full-bleed background behind the headline
images.alt_text: Modern double garage with a Merlin opener being serviced by a technician
images.prompt: Wide cinematic shot of a modern Australian suburban double garage with a sectional door partly open; a friendly uniformed technician inspects a ceiling-mounted garage door opener motor; ladder and tidy tools nearby; warm soft morning light. Keep the left third visually calm and uncluttered for text overlay.

images.alt_text: Technician repairing a ceiling-mounted garage door opener motor
images.prompt: Close-up of a male technician in a plain navy uniform repairing a ceiling-mounted SECTIONAL garage door opener motor head; he holds a screwdriver and a multimeter, the motor's plastic cover removed to reveal the circuit board and drive gear; bright residential garage interior with a white sectional panel door softly out of focus behind. Focus on the hands and the motor unit. The genuine 'MERLIN' brand wordmark is clearly and correctly visible on the motor housing, spelled exactly M-E-R-L-I-N. Photorealistic, natural daylight, landscape 16:9, exactly one garage door, architecturally correct, no extra side openings, no other brand logos, no stray text.
references: A Merlin sectional ceiling-rail opener motor head (e.g. Merlin Professional / Commander) showing the MERLIN wordmark, so the motor shape AND the logo are accurate
Optionally a photo of an opener with its cover off showing the control board

images.purpose: Service card — Roller Door Opener Repairs / tuning
images.alt_text: Technician servicing a residential roller garage door and its drive motor
images.prompt: A residential ROLLER garage door — a single curved corrugated steel curtain that coils into a round roller drum across the top of the opening (this is NOT a flat sectional panel door and NOT a ceiling-rail opener). The door is rolled half open showing the curling curtain. A technician stands to one side adjusting the compact tubular roller-door drive motor fitted at the end of the roller drum, using a small hand tool. The genuine 'MERLIN' brand wordmark is visible on the roller-door motor unit, spelled exactly M-E-R-L-I-N. Bright daylight at a suburban Australian home. Photorealistic, landscape 16:9, exactly ONE roller door, architecturally correct, no flat panelled door, no extra openings on the side, no other brand logos, no stray text.
references: A real photo of an Australian ROLLER garage door clearly showing the corrugated curtain rolling up into the round drum at the top (this is the most important reference — it stops the model drawing a flat sectional door)
The Merlin SilentDrive roller-door motor (MR855MYQ / MR655MYQ) showing the MERLIN wordmark, so the drum motor shape and logo are right

images.purpose: Service card — Remotes, Keypads & myQ setup
images.alt_text: Garage door remotes, a wall keypad, and a phone app on a workbench
images.prompt: Clean top-down flat-lay on a light workbench: two compact garage-door remote handsets, a wall-mounted wireless entry keypad, and a smartphone showing a simple garage-control app with a single open/close control. The genuine 'MERLIN' brand wordmark is clearly visible on the remote handsets and on the keypad, spelled exactly M-E-R-L-I-N. Soft even daylight, tidy composition. Photorealistic, landscape 16:9, legible Merlin branding only, no other brand logos, no stray text.
references: A Merlin remote handset (e.g. E945M) and a Merlin wireless keypad showing the MERLIN wordmark, so the device shapes and logo are accurate
Optionally a phone showing the myQ-style app screen

images.alt_text: Drive belt, chain, sprocket and trolley hardware of a garage opener
images.prompt: Macro close-up of the drive components of a ceiling-mounted SECTIONAL garage opener rail: the rubber drive belt and a metal chain running over a sprocket, the plastic trolley/carriage on the rail, plus a couple of spare sprockets and L-brackets laid on a clean workbench beside it. The opener motor head at the end of the rail carries the genuine 'MERLIN' brand wordmark, spelled exactly M-E-R-L-I-N. Mechanical, tidy, crisp focus, natural light. Photorealistic, landscape 16:9, no other brand logos, no stray text.
references: A Merlin opener showing the motor head with the MERLIN wordmark plus the rail, belt or chain, sprocket and plastic trolley carriage
Loose replacement parts (sprockets, trolley, brackets) for the laid-out detail

images.alt_text: Technician checking the photo-eye safety sensor on a garage door
images.prompt: A technician kneeling at the base of an open residential SECTIONAL garage door, checking one of the two small photo-eye infra-red safety sensors mounted low on the door track near the floor, hand near the little sensor lens; the ceiling opener rail and motor head are visible above the open door, the motor carrying the genuine 'MERLIN' brand wordmark spelled exactly M-E-R-L-I-N. Bright, reassuring daylight. Photorealistic, landscape 16:9, exactly one garage door, no extra openings, no other brand logos, no stray text.
references: A close photo of garage-door photo-eye safety sensors (the small paired infra-red sensors near the floor on the tracks)
A Merlin opener motor head showing the MERLIN wordmark for the unit above the door

images.purpose: Service card — New Merlin Opener Install
images.alt_text: Technician installing a new ceiling-rail garage door opener
images.prompt: A technician on a step ladder installing a NEW ceiling-rail SECTIONAL garage door opener: fixing the motor head and its rail to the garage ceiling with a cordless drill, the rail running from the motor to the top of a white sectional panel door. A new opener box and remotes sit on the floor below. The genuine 'MERLIN' brand wordmark is clearly visible on both the motor head and the cardboard opener box, spelled exactly M-E-R-L-I-N. Bright, clean residential garage. Photorealistic, landscape 16:9, exactly one garage door, no extra openings, no other brand logos, no stray text.
references: A Merlin sectional ceiling-rail opener kit (motor head plus rail) with the MERLIN wordmark, shown installed on a garage ceiling
A Merlin opener retail box showing the MERLIN branding

images.purpose: Why-us section feature image (shown in a 4:3 frame with badges overlaid)
images.where_used: src/components/sections/WhyUs.tsx — framed image with overlaid badges
images.alt_text: Confident uniformed garage door technician in front of a modern home
images.prompt: Confident, friendly uniformed garage door technician standing in front of a modern Australian home's double garage, arms relaxed, light smile; clean plain uniform with no visible logos; bright natural daylight; leave the lower portion slightly calmer for overlaid badges.

images.purpose: FAQ support image + final CTA banner image
images.alt_text: Friendly customer support specialist helping a homeowner by phone
images.prompt: Friendly customer support specialist at a tidy desk with a headset and laptop, warm and approachable, helping a homeowner; bright modern office; clean and trustworthy, no on-screen text.
